{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Tamatsuoka Shrine (玉津岡神社) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Tamatsuoka-jinja, keidai.jpg|280px]]
境内 | Address = [[Kyoto Prefecture]], [[Tsuzuki District]], [[Ide Town]], Ide Higashigaito 63 | deity = {{ill|Shitateruhime|ja|下照比賣命|12=draft}} | type = Former [[Gōsha]] | consecration_year = | Main Hall = | Other names = | Festivals = April 3, October 16 | Ceremonies = | Latitude Degrees = 34 | Latitude Minutes = 48 | Latitude Seconds = 15.1 | N/S = N | Longitude Degrees = 135 | Longitude Minutes = 49 | Longitude Seconds = 11.5 | E/W = E | Map Country Code = JP | Map = Japan Kyoto | Label Position = left }} [[File:Tamatsuoka-jinja, torii.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|境内入り口}}]] '''Tamatsuoka Shrine''' (玉津岡神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Ide Town]], [[Kyoto Prefecture]]. Its historical rank under the [[Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|modern shrine ranking system]] is that of a [[Gōsha]]. == Deities == The deities enshrined are as followsKoji Inui, *Ide Sadaijin Tachibana no Moroe and the Ide Township – The History and Culture of the Village of Ide*, Tamatsuoka Shrine, 1993, p.51: ; Main deity :* {{ill|Shitateruhime|ja|下照比賣命|12=draft}} ; Other Deities : The six deities of TamatsuokaMasayoshi Ōnishi, *Tamatsuoka Shrine Summary*, Tamatsuoka Shrine, 1984, p.4: :* {{ill|Shitateruhime|ja|下照比賣命|12=draft}} :* {{ill|Ame-no-Koyane|ja|天兒屋根命|12=draft}} :* {{ill|Sukunahikona|ja|少彦名命|1=Sukunabikona|12=draft}} :* {{ill|Susanoo|ja|素戔嗚命|12=draft}} :* {{ill|Ajisukitakahikone|ja|味耜高彦根命|12=draft}} :* {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|12=draft}} == History == In the first year of [[Emperor Kinmei]] (540 CE), {{ill|Shitateruhime|ja|下照比賣命|12=draft}} is said to have descended to the southern area of Tamatsuoka, where a shrine called "Tamaoka no Yashiro" was established in her honor. Over the years, the shrine was renamed "Tamaoka Kasuga Shrine" and later "Hachioji Shrine" during the Edo period, eventually becoming known as Tamatsuoka Shrine. In 1878, the main Hachioji Shrine (on the site of Tamaoka no Yashiro) consolidated five shrines: Hachioji Shrine (Tamaoka), Kasuga Shrine (Nishigaito), Tanaka Shrine (Miyanomae), Yasaka Shrine (Nishimaeda), and Tenjin Shrine (Tamanoi). The latter, Tenjin Shrine, originally known as Mukumoto Tenjin Shrine, was founded in 731 CE by {{ill|Tachibana no Moroe|ja|橘諸兄|12=draft}}. The shrine grounds also include a Tachibana Shrine dedicated to Tachibana no Moroe and one of his descendants, {{ill|Kusunoki Masashige|ja|楠木正成|12=draft}}. The shrine crest features a "flowing yamabuki (kerria)," which is associated with the family crest of Kusunoki Masashige. The main deity, Shitateruhime, is regarded as a "household harmony deity." The Edo-period main hall, constructed in the [[Kasuga-zukuri]] style, is a [[Kyoto Prefectural Cultural Property]]. The surrounding forest is also designated as a Cultural Property Environmental Preservation AreaHistorical Resource Survey Committee, History Exploration Division, *Ide Town History Tourism Guidebook – Walking Through Ide Village*, Ide Town Chamber of Commerce, 1995, p.9. == Grounds == Tamatsuoka-jinja, honden.jpg|Main Hall (back right, Kyoto Prefectural Cultural Property) Tamatsuoka-jinja, nakamon.jpg|Inner Gate Tamatsuoka-jinja, haiden.jpg|Worship Hall Tamatsuoka-jinja, massha.jpg|Sub-shrine Daijingu (Kyoto Prefectural Cultural Property) == Cultural Properties == === Kyoto Prefectural Cultural Properties === * Tangible Cultural Properties: ''' Two structures at Tamatsuoka Shrine (Buildings) – Registered April 14, 1984: '''* Main Hall – Edo period '''* Sub-shrine Daijingu – Edo period == Access == The shrine is approximately 2.0 kilometers from [[Tamamizu Station]] on the [[JR Nara Line]], about a 30-minute walk[http://www.town.ide.kyoto.jp/rekishi_kanko/kankou_meisyo/1417411132279.html Tamatsuoka Shrine] Ide Town Website. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * {{Commonscat-inline|Tamatsuoka-jinja}} {{pref-stub|pref=Kyoto}} {{shinto-stub}} {{draft categories| [[Category:completion tier 4]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Tamatsuoka Shrine]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Ide, Kyoto]] [[Category:History of Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Go-sha]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:New]] }} [[sn:Tamatsuoka Shrine]] [[ja:玉津岡神社]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Tamatsuoka Shrine}}

The latter, Tenjin Shrine, originally known as Mukumoto Tenjin Shrine, was founded in 731 CE by {{ill|Tachibana no Moroe|ja|橘諸兄|12=draft}}. The shrine grounds also include a Tachibana Shrine dedicated to Tachibana no Moroe and one of his descendants, {{ill|Kusunoki Masashige|ja|楠木正成|12=draft}}. The shrine crest features a "flowing yamabuki (kerria)," which is associated with the family crest of Kusunoki Masashige. The main deity, Shitateruhime, is regarded as a "household harmony deity." The Edo-period main hall, constructed in the [[Kasuga-zukuri]] style, is a [[Kyoto Prefectural Cultural Property]]. The surrounding forest is also designated as a Cultural Property Environmental Preservation AreaHistorical Resource Survey Committee, History Exploration Division, *Ide Town History Tourism Guidebook – Walking Through Ide Village*, Ide Town Chamber of Commerce, 1995, p.9. == Grounds == Tamatsuoka-jinja, honden.jpg|Main Hall (back right, Kyoto Prefectural Cultural Property) Tamatsuoka-jinja, nakamon.jpg|Inner Gate Tamatsuoka-jinja, haiden.jpg|Worship Hall Tamatsuoka-jinja, massha.jpg|Sub-shrine Daijingu (Kyoto Prefectural Cultural Property) == Cultural Properties == === Kyoto Prefectural Cultural Properties === * Tangible Cultural Properties: ''' Two structures at Tamatsuoka Shrine (Buildings) – Registered April 14, 1984: '''* Main Hall – Edo period '''* Sub-shrine Daijingu – Edo period == Access == The shrine is approximately 2.0 kilometers from [[Tamamizu Station]] on the [[JR Nara Line]], about a 30-minute walk[http://www.town.ide.kyoto.jp/rekishi_kanko/kankou_meisyo/1417411132279.html Tamatsuoka Shrine] Ide Town Website. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * {{Commonscat-inline|Tamatsuoka-jinja}} {{pref-stub|pref=Kyoto}} {{shinto-stub}} {{draft categories| [[Category:completion tier 4]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Tamatsuoka Shrine]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Ide, Kyoto]] [[Category:History of Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Go-sha]] [[Category:New]] }} [[sn:Tamatsuoka Shrine]] [[ja:玉津岡神社]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Tamatsuoka Shrine}}

The surrounding forest is also designated as a Cultural Property Environmental Preservation AreaHistorical Resource Survey Committee, History Exploration Division, *Ide Town History Tourism Guidebook – Walking Through Ide Village*, Ide Town Chamber of Commerce, 1995, p.9. == Grounds == Tamatsuoka-jinja, honden.jpg|Main Hall (back right, Kyoto Prefectural Cultural Property) Tamatsuoka-jinja, nakamon.jpg|Inner Gate Tamatsuoka-jinja, haiden.jpg|Worship Hall Tamatsuoka-jinja, massha.jpg|Sub-shrine Daijingu (Kyoto Prefectural Cultural Property) == Cultural Properties == === Kyoto Prefectural Cultural Properties === * Tangible Cultural Properties: ''' Two structures at Tamatsuoka Shrine (Buildings) – Registered April 14, 1984: '''* Main Hall – Edo period '''* Sub-shrine Daijingu – Edo period == Access == The shrine is approximately 2.0 kilometers from [[Tamamizu Station]] on the [[JR Nara Line]], about a 30-minute walk[http://www.town.ide.kyoto.jp/rekishi_kanko/kankou_meisyo/1417411132279.html Tamatsuoka Shrine] Ide Town Website. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * {{Commonscat-inline|Tamatsuoka-jinja}} {{pref-stub|pref=Kyoto}} {{shinto-stub}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Tamatsuoka Shrine]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Ide, Kyoto]] [[Category:History of Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Go-sha]] [[Category:New]] }} [[sn:Tamatsuoka Shrine]] [[ja:玉津岡神社]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Tamatsuoka Shrine}}

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Tamatsuoka Shrine (玉津岡神社) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Tamatsuoka-jinja, keidai.jpg|280px]]
境内 | Address = [[Kyoto Prefecture]], [[Tsuzuki District]], [[Ide Town]], Ide Higashigaito 63 | deity = {{ill|Shitateruhime|ja|下照比賣命|12=draft|12=draft}} | type = Former [[Gōsha]] | consecration_year = | Main Hall = | Other names = | Festivals = April 3, October 16 | Ceremonies = | Latitude Degrees = 34 | Latitude Minutes = 48 | Latitude Seconds = 15.1 | N/S = N | Longitude Degrees = 135 | Longitude Minutes = 49 | Longitude Seconds = 11.5 | E/W = E | Map Country Code = JP | Map = Japan Kyoto | Label Position = left }} [[File:Tamatsuoka-jinja, torii.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|境内入り口}}]] '''Tamatsuoka Shrine''' (玉津岡神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Ide Town]], [[Kyoto Prefecture]]. Its historical Rank under the [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|modern shrine Ranking system]] is that of a [[Gōsha]]. == Deities == The deities enshrined are as followsKoji Inui, *Ide Sadaijin Tachibana no Moroe and the Ide Township – The History and Culture of the Village of Ide*, Tamatsuoka Shrine, 1993, p.51: ; Main deity :* {{ill|Shitateruhime|ja|下照比賣命|12=draft|12=draft}} ; Other Deities : The six deities of TamatsuokaMasayoshi Ōnishi, *Tamatsuoka Shrine Summary*, Tamatsuoka Shrine, 1984, p.4: :* {{ill|Shitateruhime|ja|下照比賣命|12=draft|12=draft}} :* {{ill|Ame-no-Koyane|ja|天兒屋根命|12=draft|12=draft}} :* {{ill|Sukunahikona|ja|少彦名命|1=Sukunabikona|12=draft|12=draft}} :* {{ill|Susanoo|ja|素戔嗚命|12=draft|12=draft}} :* {{ill|Ajisukitakahikone|ja|味耜高彦根命|12=draft|12=draft}} :* {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|12=draft|12=draft}} == History == In the first year of [[Emperor Kinmei]] (540 CE), {{ill|Shitateruhime|ja|下照比賣命|12=draft|12=draft}} is said to have descended to the southern area of Tamatsuoka, where a shrine called "Tamaoka no Yashiro" was established in her honor. Over the years, the shrine was renamed "Tamaoka Kasuga Shrine" and later "Hachioji Shrine" during the Edo period, eventually becoming known as Tamatsuoka Shrine. In 1878, the main Hachioji Shrine (on the site of Tamaoka no Yashiro) consolidated five shrines: Hachioji Shrine (Tamaoka), Kasuga Shrine (Nishigaito), Tanaka Shrine (Miyanomae), Yasaka Shrine (Nishimaeda), and Tenjin Shrine (Tamanoi). The latter, Tenjin Shrine, originally known as Mukumoto Tenjin Shrine, was founded in 731 CE by {{ill|Tachibana no Moroe|ja|橘諸兄|12=draft|12=draft}}. The shrine grounds also include a Tachibana Shrine dedicated to Tachibana no Moroe and one of his descendants, {{ill|Kusunoki Masashige|ja|楠木正成|12=draft|12=draft}}. The shrine crest features a "flowing yamabuki (kerria)," which is associated with the family crest of Kusunoki Masashige. The main deity, Shitateruhime, is regarded as a "household harmony deity." The Edo-period main hall, constructed in the [[Kasuga-zukuri]] style, is a [[Kyoto Prefectural Cultural Property]]. The surrounding forest is also designated as a Cultural Property Environmental Preservation AreaHistorical Resource Survey Committee, History Exploration Division, *Ide Town History Tourism Guidebook – Walking Through Ide Village*, Ide Town Chamber of Commerce, 1995, p.9. == Grounds == Tamatsuoka-jinja, honden.jpg|Main Hall (back right, Kyoto Prefectural Cultural Property) Tamatsuoka-jinja, nakamon.jpg|Inner Gate Tamatsuoka-jinja, haiden.jpg|Worship Hall Tamatsuoka-jinja, massha.jpg|Sub-shrine Daijingu (Kyoto Prefectural Cultural Property) == Cultural Properties == === Kyoto Prefectural Cultural Properties === * Tangible Cultural Properties: ''' Two structures at Tamatsuoka Shrine (Buildings) – Registered April 14, 1984: '''* Main Hall – Edo period '''* Sub-shrine Daijingu – Edo period == Access == The shrine is approximately 2.0 kilometers from [[Tamamizu Station]] on the [[JR Nara Line]], about a 30-minute walk[http://www.town.ide.kyoto.jp/rekishi_kanko/kankou_meisyo/1417411132279.html Tamatsuoka Shrine] Ide Town Website. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * {{Commonscat-inline|Tamatsuoka-jinja}} {{pref-stub-Kyoto}} {{shinto-stub}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Tamatsuoka Shrine]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Ide, Kyoto]] [[Category:History of Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Go-sha]] [[Category:New]] }} [[sn:Tamatsuoka Shrine]] [[ja:玉津岡神社]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Tamatsuoka Shrine}}

revision.comment: 14 revisions imported: Bot: import full history from ja:玉津岡神社 up to rev 98461564
revision.text:
{{神社 |名称=玉津岡神社 |画像=[[File:Tamatsuoka-jinja, keidai.jpg|280px]]
境内 |所在地= [[京都府]][[綴喜郡]][[井手町]]井出東垣内63 |祭神=[[シタテルヒメ|下照比賣命]] |社格= 旧[[郷社]] |創建= |本殿= |別名= |例祭=4月3日・10月16日 |神事= | 緯度度 = 34 | 緯度分 = 48 | 緯度秒 = 15.1 | N(北緯)及びS(南緯) = N | 経度度 = 135 |経度分 = 49 | 経度秒 = 11.5 | E(東経)及びW(西経) = E | 地図国コード = JP |地図=Japan Kyoto |ラベル位置=left }} [[File:Tamatsuoka-jinja, torii.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|境内入り口}}]] '''玉津岡神社'''（たまつおかじんじゃ）は、[[京都府]][[綴喜郡]][[井手町]]にある[[神社]]。[[近代社格制度|旧社格]]は[[郷社]]。 == 祭神 == 祭神は次の通り乾幸二『井手左大臣橘諸兄公と井堤（井出）郷　－古代よりの歴史と文化が薫る井手の里－』玉津岡神社、1993年、p.51。 ; 主祭神 :*[[シタテルヒメ|下照比賣命]]（したてるひめのみこと） ; 大神 : 玉津岡六柱の大神大西正宜『玉津岡神社略記』玉津岡神社、1984年、p.4。 :* 下照比賣命 :* [[天兒屋根命]] :* [[少彦名命]] :* [[素戔嗚命]] :* [[味耜高彦根命]] :* [[菅原道真]]公 == 歴史 == [[欽明天皇]]元年（540年）、玉津岡の南に[[シタテルヒメ|下照比賣命]]が降臨、そこでお祭りをした「玉岡の社」が玉津岡神社の起源。「玉岡の社」は「玉岡春日社」、江戸時代に「八王子社」と称号を変え、現在は玉津岡神社となる。1878年（明治11年）には、八王子社（玉津岡鎮座）、春日社（西垣内鎮座）、田中社（宮の前鎮座）、八阪社（西前田鎮座）、天神社（玉の井鎮座）の五社が八王子社殿（玉岡の社）に合祭している。天神社（玉の井鎮座）は椋本天神社を移したもので、その創祀は天平3年（731年）9月であり、創祀者は[[橘諸兄]]公とされる。境内には[[橘諸兄]]とその一族の一人[[楠木正成]]公を合祭した橘神社がある。また玉津岡神社の[[社紋]]は「流れ山吹」であるが、これは楠木正成の家紋「菊水」と同じものともされている。ちなみに主神の下照比賣命は「家内和合の神」である。江戸時代に[[春日造]]で建てられた本殿は[[京都府指定・登録文化財一覧|京都府登録文化財]]、[[鎮守の森]]は文化財環境保全地区となっている。地域資源調査委員会 歴史探訪部会編『井手町 歴史観光ガイドブック 井手の里を歩く』井手町商工会、1995年、p.9。 == 境内 == Tamatsuoka-jinja, honden.jpg|本殿（右奥、京都府登録文化財） Tamatsuoka-jinja, nakamon.jpg|中門 Tamatsuoka-jinja, haiden.jpg|拝殿 Tamatsuoka-jinja, massha.jpg|末社大神宮社（京都府登録文化財） == 文化財 == === 京都府登録文化財 === * 有形文化財 ** 玉津岡神社 2棟（建造物） - 1984年（昭和59年）4月14日登録。 *** 本殿 - 江戸時代。 *** 末社大神宮社 - 江戸時代。 == 交通アクセス == [[JR奈良線]][[玉水駅]]より約2.0キロメートル、徒歩約30分[http://www.town.ide.kyoto.jp/rekishi_kanko/kankou_meisyo/1417411132279.html 玉津岡神社] 井手町ウェブサイト。 == 脚注 == {{Reflist}} == 外部リンク == * {{Commonscat-inline|Tamatsuoka-jinja}} {{pref-stub|pref=京都府}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:たまつおかしんしや}} [[Category:京都府の神社]] [[Category:井手町の建築物]] [[Category:井手町の歴史]] [[Category:京都府の旧郷社]]

The shrine grounds also include a Tachibana Shrine dedicated to Tachibana no Moroe and one of his descendants, {{ill|Kusunoki Masashige|ja|楠木正成|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. The shrine crest features a "flowing yamabuki (kerria)," which is associated with the family crest of Kusunoki Masashige. The main deity, Shitateruhime, is regarded as a "household harmony deity." The surrounding forest is also designated as a Cultural Property Environmental Preservation AreaHistorical Resource Survey Committee, History Exploration Division, *Ide Town History Tourism Guidebook – Walking Through Ide Village*, Ide Town Chamber of Commerce, 1995, p.9. == Grounds == Tamatsuoka-jinja, honden.jpg|Main Hall (back right, Kyoto Prefectural Cultural Property) Tamatsuoka-jinja, nakamon.jpg|Inner Gate Tamatsuoka-jinja, haiden.jpg|Worship Hall Tamatsuoka-jinja, massha.jpg|Sub-shrine Daijingu (Kyoto Prefectural Cultural Property) == Cultural Properties == === Kyoto Prefectural Cultural Properties === * Tangible Cultural Properties: ''' Two structures at Tamatsuoka Shrine (Buildings) – Registered April 14, 1984: '''* Main Hall – Edo period '''* Sub-shrine Daijingu – Edo period == Access == The shrine is approximately 2.0 kilometers from [[Tamamizu Station]] on the [[JR Nara Line]], about a 30-minute walk[http://www.town.ide.kyoto.jp/rekishi_kanko/kankou_meisyo/1417411132279.html Tamatsuoka Shrine] Ide Town Website. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * {{Commonscat-inline|Tamatsuoka-jinja}} {{pref-stub-Kyoto}} {{shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|玉津岡神社|version=98461564|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Tamatsuoka Shrine]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Ide, Kyoto]] [[Category:History of Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Go-sha]] }} [[sn:Tamatsuoka Shrine]] [[ja:玉津岡神社]]

Its historical Rank under the [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|modern shrine Ranking system]] is that of a [[Gōsha]]. == Deities == The deities enshrined are as followsKoji Inui, *Ide Sadaijin Tachibana no Moroe and the Ide Township – The History and Culture of the Village of Ide*, Tamatsuoka Shrine, 1993, p.51: ; Main deity :* {{ill|Shitateruhime|ja|下照比賣命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shitateruhime|qq=}} ; Other Deities : The six deities of TamatsuokaMasayoshi Ōnishi, *Tamatsuoka Shrine Summary*, Tamatsuoka Shrine, 1984, p.4: :* {{ill|Shitateruhime|ja|下照比賣命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shitateruhime|qq=}} :* {{ill|Ame-no-Koyane|ja|天兒屋根命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Koyane|qq=}} :* {{ill|Sukunahikona|ja|少彦名命|1=Sukunabikona|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunahikona|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunabikona|qq=}} :* {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素戔嗚命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=}} :* {{ill|Ajisukitakahikone|ja|味耜高彦根命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ajisukitakahikone|qq=}} :* {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|qq=}} == History == In the first year of [[Emperor Kinmei]] (540 CE), {{ill|Shitateruhime|ja|下照比賣命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shitateruhime|qq=}} is said to have descended to the southern area of Tamatsuoka, where a shrine called "Tamaoka no Yashiro" was established in her honor. Over the years, the shrine was renamed "Tamaoka Kasuga Shrine" and later "Hachioji Shrine" during the Edo period, eventually becoming known as Tamatsuoka Shrine. In 1878, the main Hachioji Shrine (on the site of Tamaoka no Yashiro) consolidated five shrines: Hachioji Shrine (Tamaoka), Kasuga Shrine (Nishigaito), Tanaka Shrine (Miyanomae), Yasaka Shrine (Nishimaeda), and Tenjin Shrine (Tamanoi). The latter, Tenjin Shrine, originally known as Mukumoto Tenjin Shrine, was founded in 731 CE by {{ill|Tachibana no Moroe|ja|橘諸兄|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tachibana no Moroe|qq=}}. The shrine grounds also include a Tachibana Shrine dedicated to Tachibana no Moroe and one of his descendants, {{ill|Kusunoki Masashige|ja|楠木正成|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kusunoki Masashige|qq=}}. The shrine crest features a "flowing yamabuki (kerria)," which is associated with the family crest of Kusunoki Masashige. The main deity, Shitateruhime, is regarded as a "household harmony deity." The Edo-period main hall, constructed in the [[Kasuga-zukuri]] style, is a [[Kyoto Prefectural Cultural Property]]. The surrounding forest is also designated as a Cultural Property Environmental Preservation AreaHistorical Resource Survey Committee, History Exploration Division, *Ide Town History Tourism Guidebook – Walking Through Ide Village*, Ide Town Chamber of Commerce, 1995, p.9. == Grounds == Tamatsuoka-jinja, honden.jpg|Main Hall (back right, Kyoto Prefectural Cultural Property) Tamatsuoka-jinja, nakamon.jpg|Inner Gate Tamatsuoka-jinja, haiden.jpg|Worship Hall Tamatsuoka-jinja, massha.jpg|Sub-shrine Daijingu (Kyoto Prefectural Cultural Property) == Cultural Properties == === Kyoto Prefectural Cultural Properties === * Tangible Cultural Properties: ''' Two structures at Tamatsuoka Shrine (Buildings) – Registered April 14, 1984: '''* Main Hall – Edo period '''* Sub-shrine Daijingu – Edo period == Access == The shrine is approximately 2.0 kilometers from [[Tamamizu Station]] on the [[JR Nara Line]], about a 30-minute walk[http://www.town.ide.kyoto.jp/rekishi_kanko/kankou_meisyo/1417411132279.html Tamatsuoka Shrine] Ide Town Website. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * {{Commonscat-inline|Tamatsuoka-jinja}} {{pref-stub-Kyoto}} {{shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|玉津岡神社|version=98461564|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Tamatsuoka Shrine]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Ide, Kyoto]] [[Category:History of Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Go-sha]] }} [[sn:Tamatsuoka Shrine]] [[ja:玉津岡神社]]

Its historical Rank under the [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|modern shrine Ranking system]] is that of a [[Gōsha]]. == Deities == The deities enshrined are as followsKoji Inui, *Ide Sadaijin Tachibana no Moroe and the Ide Township – The History and Culture of the Village of Ide*, Tamatsuoka Shrine, 1993, p.51: ; Main deity :* {{ill|Shitateruhime|ja|下照比賣命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shitateruhime|qq=}} ; Other Deities : The six deities of TamatsuokaMasayoshi Ōnishi, *Tamatsuoka Shrine Summary*, Tamatsuoka Shrine, 1984, p.4: :* {{ill|Shitateruhime|ja|下照比賣命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shitateruhime|qq=}} :* {{ill|Ame-no-Koyane|ja|天兒屋根命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Koyane|qq=}} :* {{ill|Sukunahikona|ja|少彦名命|1=Sukunabikona|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunahikona|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunabikona|qq=}} :* {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素戔嗚命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=}} :* {{ill|Ajisukitakahikone|ja|味耜高彦根命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ajisukitakahikone|qq=}} :* {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|qq=}} == History == In the first year of [[Emperor Kinmei]] (540 CE), {{ill|Shitateruhime|ja|下照比賣命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shitateruhime|qq=}} is said to have descended to the southern area of Tamatsuoka, where a shrine called "Tamaoka no Yashiro" was established in her honor. Over the years, the shrine was renamed "Tamaoka Kasuga Shrine" and later "Hachioji Shrine" during the Edo period, eventually becoming known as Tamatsuoka Shrine. In 1878, the main Hachioji Shrine (on the site of Tamaoka no Yashiro) consolidated five shrines: Hachioji Shrine (Tamaoka), Kasuga Shrine (Nishigaito), Tanaka Shrine (Miyanomae), Yasaka Shrine (Nishimaeda), and Tenjin Shrine (Tamanoi). The latter, Tenjin Shrine, originally known as Mukumoto Tenjin Shrine, was founded in 731 CE by [[:en:Tachibana no Moroe|Tachibana no Moroe]]. The shrine grounds also include a Tachibana Shrine dedicated to Tachibana no Moroe and one of his descendants, [[:en:Kusunoki Masashige|Kusunoki Masashige]]. The shrine crest features a "flowing yamabuki (kerria)," which is associated with the family crest of Kusunoki Masashige. The main deity, Shitateruhime, is regarded as a "household harmony deity." The Edo-period main hall, constructed in the [[Kasuga-zukuri]] style, is a [[Kyoto Prefectural Cultural Property]]. The surrounding forest is also designated as a Cultural Property Environmental Preservation AreaHistorical Resource Survey Committee, History Exploration Division, *Ide Town History Tourism Guidebook – Walking Through Ide Village*, Ide Town Chamber of Commerce, 1995, p.9. == Grounds == Tamatsuoka-jinja, honden.jpg|Main Hall (back right, Kyoto Prefectural Cultural Property) Tamatsuoka-jinja, nakamon.jpg|Inner Gate Tamatsuoka-jinja, haiden.jpg|Worship Hall Tamatsuoka-jinja, massha.jpg|Sub-shrine Daijingu (Kyoto Prefectural Cultural Property) == Cultural Properties == === Kyoto Prefectural Cultural Properties === * Tangible Cultural Properties: ''' Two structures at Tamatsuoka Shrine (Buildings) – Registered April 14, 1984: '''* Main Hall – Edo period '''* Sub-shrine Daijingu – Edo period == Access == The shrine is approximately 2.0 kilometers from [[Tamamizu Station]] on the [[JR Nara Line]], about a 30-minute walk[http://www.town.ide.kyoto.jp/rekishi_kanko/kankou_meisyo/1417411132279.html Tamatsuoka Shrine] Ide Town Website. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * {{Commonscat-inline|Tamatsuoka-jinja}} {{pref-stub-Kyoto}} {{shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|玉津岡神社|version=98461564|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Go-sha]] }} [[sn:Tamatsuoka Shrine]] [[ja:玉津岡神社]] [[Category:井手町の建築物]]

Over the years, the shrine was renamed "Tamaoka Kasuga Shrine" and later "Hachioji Shrine" during the Edo period, eventually becoming known as Tamatsuoka Shrine. In 1878, the main Hachioji Shrine (on the site of Tamaoka no Yashiro) consolidated five shrines: Hachioji Shrine (Tamaoka), Kasuga Shrine (Nishigaito), Tanaka Shrine (Miyanomae), Yasaka Shrine (Nishimaeda), and Tenjin Shrine (Tamanoi). The latter, Tenjin Shrine, originally known as Mukumoto Tenjin Shrine, was founded in 731 CE by [[:en:Tachibana no Moroe|Tachibana no Moroe]]. The shrine grounds also include a Tachibana Shrine dedicated to Tachibana no Moroe and one of his descendants, [[:en:Kusunoki Masashige|Kusunoki Masashige]]. The shrine crest features a "flowing yamabuki (kerria)," which is associated with the family crest of Kusunoki Masashige. The main deity, Shitateruhime, is regarded as a "household harmony deity." The Edo-period main hall, constructed in the [[Kasuga-zukuri]] style, is a [[Kyoto Prefectural Cultural Property]]. The surrounding forest is also designated as a Cultural Property Environmental Preservation AreaHistorical Resource Survey Committee, History Exploration Division, *Ide Town History Tourism Guidebook – Walking Through Ide Village*, Ide Town Chamber of Commerce, 1995, p.9. == Grounds == Tamatsuoka-jinja, honden.jpg|Main Hall (back right, Kyoto Prefectural Cultural Property) Tamatsuoka-jinja, nakamon.jpg|Inner Gate Tamatsuoka-jinja, haiden.jpg|Worship Hall Tamatsuoka-jinja, massha.jpg|Sub-shrine Daijingu (Kyoto Prefectural Cultural Property) == Cultural Properties == === Kyoto Prefectural Cultural Properties === * Tangible Cultural Properties: ''' Two structures at Tamatsuoka Shrine (Buildings) – Registered April 14, 1984: '''* Main Hall – Edo period '''* Sub-shrine Daijingu – Edo period == Access == The shrine is approximately 2.0 kilometers from [[Tamamizu Station]] on the [[JR Nara Line]], about a 30-minute walk[http://www.town.ide.kyoto.jp/rekishi_kanko/kankou_meisyo/1417411132279.html Tamatsuoka Shrine] Ide Town Website. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * {{Commonscat-inline|Tamatsuoka-jinja}} {{pref-stub-Kyoto}} {{shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|玉津岡神社|version=98461564|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Go-sha]] }} [[sn:Tamatsuoka Shrine]] [[ja:玉津岡神社]] [[Category:Buildings in Ide, Kyoto]] [[Category:井手町の歴史]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Kyoto Prefecture]] {{Commons category|Tamatsuoka-jinja}}

Its historical Rank under the [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|modern shrine Ranking system]] is that of a [[Gōsha]]. == Deities == The deities enshrined are as followsKoji Inui, *Ide Sadaijin Tachibana no Moroe and the Ide Township – The History and Culture of the Village of Ide*, Tamatsuoka Shrine, 1993, p.51: ; Main deity :* {{ill|Shitateruhime|ja|下照比賣命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shitateruhime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ; Other Deities : The six deities of TamatsuokaMasayoshi Ōnishi, *Tamatsuoka Shrine Summary*, Tamatsuoka Shrine, 1984, p.4: :* {{ill|Shitateruhime|ja|下照比賣命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shitateruhime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} :* {{ill|Ame-no-Koyane|ja|天兒屋根命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Koyane|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天児屋命}} :* {{ill|Sukunahikona|ja|少彦名命|1=Sukunabikona|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunahikona|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunabikona|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スクナビコナ}} :* {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素戔嗚命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}} :* {{ill|Ajisukitakahikone|ja|味耜高彦根命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ajisukitakahikone|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to アヂスキタカヒコネ}} :* {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} == History == In the first year of [[:en:Emperor Kinmei|Emperor Kinmei]] (540 CE), {{ill|Shitateruhime|ja|下照比賣命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shitateruhime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} is said to have descended to the southern area of Tamatsuoka, where a shrine called "Tamaoka no Yashiro" was established in her honor. Over the years, the shrine was renamed "Tamaoka Kasuga Shrine" and later "Hachioji Shrine" during the Edo period, eventually becoming known as Tamatsuoka Shrine. In 1878, the main Hachioji Shrine (on the site of Tamaoka no Yashiro) consolidated five shrines: Hachioji Shrine (Tamaoka), Kasuga Shrine (Nishigaito), Tanaka Shrine (Miyanomae), Yasaka Shrine (Nishimaeda), and Tenjin Shrine (Tamanoi). The latter, Tenjin Shrine, originally known as Mukumoto Tenjin Shrine, was founded in 731 CE by [[:en:Tachibana no Moroe|Tachibana no Moroe]]. The shrine grounds also include a Tachibana Shrine dedicated to Tachibana no Moroe and one of his descendants, [[:en:Kusunoki Masashige|Kusunoki Masashige]]. The shrine crest features a "flowing yamabuki (kerria)," which is associated with the family crest of Kusunoki Masashige. The main deity, Shitateruhime, is regarded as a "household harmony deity." The Edo-period main hall, constructed in the [[Kasuga-zukuri]] style, is a [[Kyoto Prefectural Cultural Property]]. The surrounding forest is also designated as a Cultural Property Environmental Preservation AreaHistorical Resource Survey Committee, History Exploration Division, *Ide Town History Tourism Guidebook – Walking Through Ide Village*, Ide Town Chamber of Commerce, 1995, p.9. == Grounds == Tamatsuoka-jinja, honden.jpg|Main Hall (back right, Kyoto Prefectural Cultural Property) Tamatsuoka-jinja, nakamon.jpg|Inner Gate Tamatsuoka-jinja, haiden.jpg|Worship Hall Tamatsuoka-jinja, massha.jpg|Sub-shrine Daijingu (Kyoto Prefectural Cultural Property) == Cultural Properties == === Kyoto Prefectural Cultural Properties === * Tangible Cultural Properties: ''' Two structures at Tamatsuoka Shrine (Buildings) – Registered April 14, 1984: '''* Main Hall – Edo period '''* Sub-shrine Daijingu – Edo period == Access == The shrine is approximately 2.0 kilometers from [[Tamamizu Station]] on the [[:en:JR Nara Line|JR Nara Line]], about a 30-minute walk[http://www.town.ide.kyoto.jp/rekishi_kanko/kankou_meisyo/1417411132279.html Tamatsuoka Shrine] Ide Town Website. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * {{Commonscat-inline|Tamatsuoka-jinja}} {{pref-stub-Kyoto}} {{shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|玉津岡神社|version=98461564|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Kyoto Prefecture]] }} [[sn:Tamatsuoka Shrine]] [[ja:玉津岡神社]] [[Category:Buildings in Ide, Kyoto]] [[Category:井手町の歴史]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Kyoto Prefecture]] {{Commons category|Tamatsuoka-jinja}}

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Tamatsuoka Shrine (玉津岡神社) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Tamatsuoka-jinja, keidai.jpg|280px]]
境内 | Address = [[:en:Kyoto Prefecture|Kyoto Prefecture]], [[Tsuzuki District]], [[Ide Town]], Ide Higashigaito 63 | deity = {{ill|Shitateruhime|ja|下照比賣命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shitateruhime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} | type = Former [[Gōsha]] | consecration_year = | Main Hall = | Other names = | Festivals = April 3, October 16 | Ceremonies = | Latitude Degrees = 34 | Latitude Minutes = 48 | Latitude Seconds = 15.1 | N/S = N | Longitude Degrees = 135 | Longitude Minutes = 49 | Longitude Seconds = 11.5 | E/W = E | Map Country Code = JP | Map = Japan Kyoto | Label Position = left }} [[File:Tamatsuoka-jinja, torii.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|境内入り口}}]] '''Tamatsuoka Shrine''' (玉津岡神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Ide Town]], [[:en:Kyoto Prefecture|Kyoto Prefecture]]. Its historical Rank under the [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|modern shrine Ranking system]] is that of a [[Gōsha]]. == Deities == The deities enshrined are as followsKoji Inui, *Ide Sadaijin Tachibana no Moroe and the Ide Township – The History and Culture of the Village of Ide*, Tamatsuoka Shrine, 1993, p.51: ; Main deity :* {{ill|Shitateruhime|ja|下照比賣命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shitateruhime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ; Other Deities : The six deities of TamatsuokaMasayoshi Ōnishi, *Tamatsuoka Shrine Summary*, Tamatsuoka Shrine, 1984, p.4: :* {{ill|Shitateruhime|ja|下照比賣命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shitateruhime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} :* {{ill|Ame-no-Koyane|ja|天兒屋根命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Koyane|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天児屋命}} :* {{ill|Sukunahikona|ja|少彦名命|1=Sukunabikona|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunahikona|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunabikona|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スクナビコナ}} :* {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素戔嗚命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}} :* {{ill|Ajisukitakahikone|ja|味耜高彦根命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ajisukitakahikone|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to アヂスキタカヒコネ}} :* {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} == History == In the first year of [[:en:Emperor Kinmei|Emperor Kinmei]] (540 CE), {{ill|Shitateruhime|ja|下照比賣命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shitateruhime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} is said to have descended to the southern area of Tamatsuoka, where a shrine called "Tamaoka no Yashiro" was established in her honor. Over the years, the shrine was renamed "Tamaoka Kasuga Shrine" and later "Hachioji Shrine" during the Edo period, eventually becoming known as Tamatsuoka Shrine. In 1878, the main Hachioji Shrine (on the site of Tamaoka no Yashiro) consolidated five shrines: Hachioji Shrine (Tamaoka), Kasuga Shrine (Nishigaito), Tanaka Shrine (Miyanomae), Yasaka Shrine (Nishimaeda), and Tenjin Shrine (Tamanoi). The latter, Tenjin Shrine, originally known as Mukumoto Tenjin Shrine, was founded in 731 CE by [[:en:Tachibana no Moroe|Tachibana no Moroe]]. The shrine grounds also include a Tachibana Shrine dedicated to Tachibana no Moroe and one of his descendants, [[:en:Kusunoki Masashige|Kusunoki Masashige]]. The shrine crest features a "flowing yamabuki (kerria)," which is associated with the family crest of Kusunoki Masashige. The main deity, Shitateruhime, is regarded as a "household harmony deity." The Edo-period main hall, constructed in the [[Kasuga-zukuri]] style, is a [[Kyoto Prefectural Cultural Property]]. The surrounding forest is also designated as a Cultural Property Environmental Preservation AreaHistorical Resource Survey Committee, History Exploration Division, *Ide Town History Tourism Guidebook – Walking Through Ide Village*, Ide Town Chamber of Commerce, 1995, p.9. == Grounds == Tamatsuoka-jinja, honden.jpg|Main Hall (back right, Kyoto Prefectural Cultural Property) Tamatsuoka-jinja, nakamon.jpg|Inner Gate Tamatsuoka-jinja, haiden.jpg|Worship Hall Tamatsuoka-jinja, massha.jpg|Sub-shrine Daijingu (Kyoto Prefectural Cultural Property) == Cultural Properties == === Kyoto Prefectural Cultural Properties === * Tangible Cultural Properties: ''' Two structures at Tamatsuoka Shrine (Buildings) – Registered April 14, 1984: '''* Main Hall – Edo period '''* Sub-shrine Daijingu – Edo period == Access == The shrine is approximately 2.0 kilometers from [[:en:Tamamizu Station|Tamamizu Station]] on the [[:en:JR Nara Line|JR Nara Line]], about a 30-minute walk[http://www.town.ide.kyoto.jp/rekishi_kanko/kankou_meisyo/1417411132279.html Tamatsuoka Shrine] Ide Town Website. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * {{Commonscat-inline|Tamatsuoka-jinja}} {{pref-stub-Kyoto}} {{shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|玉津岡神社|version=98461564|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Kyoto Prefecture]] }} [[sn:Tamatsuoka Shrine]] [[ja:玉津岡神社]] [[Category:Buildings in Ide, Kyoto]] [[Category:Municipal History of Ide]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Kyoto Prefecture]] {{Commons category|Tamatsuoka-jinja}}

The latter, Tenjin Shrine, originally known as Mukumoto Tenjin Shrine, was founded in 731 CE by [[:en:Tachibana no Moroe|Tachibana no Moroe]]. The shrine grounds also include a Tachibana Shrine dedicated to Tachibana no Moroe and one of his descendants, [[:en:Kusunoki Masashige|Kusunoki Masashige]]. The shrine crest features a "flowing yamabuki (kerria)," which is associated with the family crest of Kusunoki Masashige. The main deity, Shitateruhime, is regarded as a "household harmony deity." revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[commons:Category:Tamatsuoka-jinja]][[ja:玉津岡神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q48763553]] Wikidata name: Tamatsuoka-jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:49, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Tamatsuoka Shrine (玉津岡神社) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:Tamatsuoka-jinja, keidai.jpg | Address = [[:en:Kyoto Prefecture|Kyoto Prefecture]], [[Tsuzuki District]], [[Ide Town]], Ide Higashigaito 63 | deity = {{ill|Shitateruhime|ja|下照比賣命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shitateruhime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} | type = Former [[Gōsha]] | consecration_year = | Main Hall = | Other names = | Festivals = April 3, October 16 | Ceremonies = | Latitude Degrees = 34 | Latitude Minutes = 48 | Latitude Seconds = 15.1 | N/S = N | Longitude Degrees = 135 | Longitude Minutes = 49 | Longitude Seconds = 11.5 | E/W = E | Map Country Code = JP | Map = Japan Kyoto | Label Position = left | location = {{ill|Ide|qid=Q1203097}} | coordinates = {{Coord|34.804194|N|135.819861|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }} [[File:Tamatsuoka-jinja, torii.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|境内入り口}}]] '''Tamatsuoka Shrine''' (玉津岡神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[Ide Town]], [[:en:Kyoto Prefecture|Kyoto Prefecture]]. Its historical Rank under the [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|modern shrine Ranking system]] is that of a [[Gōsha]].
== Deities == The deities enshrined are as follows<ref name="inui1993">Koji Inui, *Ide Sadaijin Tachibana no Moroe and the Ide Township – The History and Culture of the Village of Ide*, Tamatsuoka Shrine, 1993, p.51</ref>:
; Main deity :* {{ill|Shitateruhime|ja|下照比賣命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shitateruhime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
; Other Deities : The six deities of Tamatsuoka<ref>Masayoshi Ōnishi, *Tamatsuoka Shrine Summary*, Tamatsuoka Shrine, 1984, p.4</ref>: :* {{ill|Shitateruhime|ja|下照比賣命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shitateruhime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} :* {{ill|Ame-no-Koyane|ja|天兒屋根命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Koyane|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天児屋命}} :* {{ill|Sukunahikona|ja|少彦名命|1=Sukunabikona|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunahikona|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunabikona|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スクナビコナ}} :* {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素戔嗚命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}} :* {{ill|Ajisukitakahikone|ja|味耜高彦根命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ajisukitakahikone|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to アヂスキタカヒコネ}} :* {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q382005}}
== History == In the first year of [[:en:Emperor Kinmei|Emperor Kinmei]] (540 CE), {{ill|Shitateruhime|ja|下照比賣命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shitateruhime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} is said to have descended to the southern area of Tamatsuoka, where a shrine called "Tamaoka no Yashiro" was established in her honor. Over the years, the shrine was renamed "Tamaoka Kasuga Shrine" and later "Hachioji Shrine" during the Edo period, eventually becoming known as Tamatsuoka Shrine.
In 1878, the main Hachioji Shrine (on the site of Tamaoka no Yashiro) consolidated five shrines: Hachioji Shrine (Tamaoka), Kasuga Shrine (Nishigaito), Tanaka Shrine (Miyanomae), Yasaka Shrine (Nishimaeda), and Tenjin Shrine (Tamanoi). The latter, Tenjin Shrine, originally known as Mukumoto Tenjin Shrine, was founded in 731 CE by [[:en:Tachibana no Moroe|Tachibana no Moroe]]<ref name="inui1993"/>.
The shrine grounds also include a Tachibana Shrine dedicated to Tachibana no Moroe and one of his descendants, [[:en:Kusunoki Masashige|Kusunoki Masashige]]. The shrine crest features a "flowing yamabuki (kerria)," which is associated with the family crest of Kusunoki Masashige. The main deity, Shitateruhime, is regarded as a "household harmony deity." The Edo-period main hall, constructed in the [[Kasuga-zukuri]] style, is a [[Kyoto Prefectural Cultural Property]]. The surrounding forest is also designated as a Cultural Property Environmental Preservation Area<ref>Historical Resource Survey Committee, History Exploration Division, *Ide Town History Tourism Guidebook – Walking Through Ide Village*, Ide Town Chamber of Commerce, 1995, p.9</ref>.
== Grounds == <gallery> Tamatsuoka-jinja, honden.jpg|Main Hall (back right, Kyoto Prefectural Cultural Property) Tamatsuoka-jinja, nakamon.jpg|Inner Gate Tamatsuoka-jinja, haiden.jpg|Worship Hall Tamatsuoka-jinja, massha.jpg|Sub-shrine Daijingu (Kyoto Prefectural Cultural Property) </gallery>
== Cultural Properties == === Kyoto Prefectural Cultural Properties === * Tangible Cultural Properties: ''' Two structures at Tamatsuoka Shrine (Buildings) – Registered April 14, 1984: '''* Main Hall – Edo period '''* Sub-shrine Daijingu – Edo period
== Access == The shrine is approximately 2.0 kilometers from [[:en:Tamamizu Station|Tamamizu Station]] on the [[:en:JR Nara Line|JR Nara Line]], about a 30-minute walk<ref>[http://www.town.ide.kyoto.jp/rekishi_kanko/kankou_meisyo/1417411132279.html Tamatsuoka Shrine] Ide Town Website</ref>.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Tamatsuoka-jinja]][[ja:玉津岡神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q48763553]] Wikidata name: Tamatsuoka-jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:49, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Tamatsuoka Shrine (玉津岡神社) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:Tamatsuoka-jinja, keidai.jpg | Address = [[:en:Kyoto Prefecture|Kyoto Prefecture]], [[Tsuzuki District]], [[Ide Town]], Ide Higashigaito 63 | deity = {{ill|Shitateruhime|ja|下照比賣命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shitateruhime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} | type = Former [[Gōsha]] | consecration_year = | Main Hall = | Other names = | Festivals = April 3, October 16 | Ceremonies = | Latitude Degrees = 34 | Latitude Minutes = 48 | Latitude Seconds = 15.1 | N/S = N | Longitude Degrees = 135 | Longitude Minutes = 49 | Longitude Seconds = 11.5 | E/W = E | Map Country Code = JP | Map = Japan Kyoto | Label Position = left | location = {{ill|Ide|qid=Q1203097}} | coordinates = {{Coord|34.804194|N|135.819861|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }} [[File:Tamatsuoka-jinja, torii.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|境内入り口}}]] '''Tamatsuoka Shrine''' (玉津岡神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[Ide Town]], [[:en:Kyoto Prefecture|Kyoto Prefecture]]. Its historical Rank under the [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|modern shrine Ranking system]] is that of a [[Gōsha]].
== Deities == The deities enshrined are as follows<ref name="inui1993">Koji Inui, *Ide Sadaijin Tachibana no Moroe and the Ide Township – The History and Culture of the Village of Ide*, Tamatsuoka Shrine, 1993, p.51</ref>:
; Main deity :* {{ill|Shitateruhime|ja|下照比賣命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shitateruhime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
; Other Deities : The six deities of Tamatsuoka<ref>Masayoshi Ōnishi, *Tamatsuoka Shrine Summary*, Tamatsuoka Shrine, 1984, p.4</ref>: :* {{ill|Shitateruhime|ja|下照比賣命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shitateruhime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} :* {{ill|Ame-no-Koyane|ja|天兒屋根命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Koyane|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天児屋命}} :* {{ill|Sukunahikona|ja|少彦名命|1=Sukunabikona|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunahikona|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunabikona|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スクナビコナ}} :* {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素戔嗚命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}} :* {{ill|Ajisukitakahikone|ja|味耜高彦根命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ajisukitakahikone|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to アヂスキタカヒコネ}} :* {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q382005}}
== History == In the first year of [[:en:Emperor Kinmei|Emperor Kinmei]] (540 CE), {{ill|Shitateruhime|ja|下照比賣命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shitateruhime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} is said to have descended to the southern area of Tamatsuoka, where a shrine called "Tamaoka no Yashiro" was established in her honor. Over the years, the shrine was renamed "Tamaoka Kasuga Shrine" and later "Hachioji Shrine" during the Edo period, eventually becoming known as Tamatsuoka Shrine.
In 1878, the main Hachioji Shrine (on the site of Tamaoka no Yashiro) consolidated five shrines: Hachioji Shrine (Tamaoka), Kasuga Shrine (Nishigaito), Tanaka Shrine (Miyanomae), Yasaka Shrine (Nishimaeda), and Tenjin Shrine (Tamanoi). The latter, Tenjin Shrine, originally known as Mukumoto Tenjin Shrine, was founded in 731 CE by [[:en:Tachibana no Moroe|Tachibana no Moroe]]<ref name="inui1993"/>.
The shrine grounds also include a Tachibana Shrine dedicated to Tachibana no Moroe and one of his descendants, [[:en:Kusunoki Masashige|Kusunoki Masashige]]. The shrine crest features a "flowing yamabuki (kerria)," which is associated with the family crest of Kusunoki Masashige. The main deity, Shitateruhime, is regarded as a "household harmony deity." The Edo-period main hall, constructed in the [[Kasuga-zukuri]] style, is a [[Kyoto Prefectural Cultural Property]]. The surrounding forest is also designated as a Cultural Property Environmental Preservation Area<ref>Historical Resource Survey Committee, History Exploration Division, *Ide Town History Tourism Guidebook – Walking Through Ide Village*, Ide Town Chamber of Commerce, 1995, p.9</ref>.
== Grounds == <gallery> Tamatsuoka-jinja, honden.jpg|Main Hall (back right, Kyoto Prefectural Cultural Property) Tamatsuoka-jinja, nakamon.jpg|Inner Gate Tamatsuoka-jinja, haiden.jpg|Worship Hall Tamatsuoka-jinja, massha.jpg|Sub-shrine Daijingu (Kyoto Prefectural Cultural Property) </gallery>
== Cultural Properties == === Kyoto Prefectural Cultural Properties === * Tangible Cultural Properties: ''' Two structures at Tamatsuoka Shrine (Buildings) – Registered April 14, 1984: '''* Main Hall – Edo period '''* Sub-shrine Daijingu – Edo period
== Access == The shrine is approximately 2.0 kilometers from [[:en:Tamamizu Station|Tamamizu Station]] on the [[:en:JR Nara Line|JR Nara Line]], about a 30-minute walk<ref>[http://www.town.ide.kyoto.jp/rekishi_kanko/kankou_meisyo/1417411132279.html Tamatsuoka Shrine] Ide Town Website</ref>.

; Other Deities : The six deities of Tamatsuoka<ref>Masayoshi Ōnishi, *Tamatsuoka Shrine Summary*, Tamatsuoka Shrine, 1984, p.4</ref>: :* {{ill|Shitateruhime|ja|下照比賣命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shitateruhime|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} :* {{ill|Ame-no-Koyane|ja|天兒屋根命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Koyane|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天児屋命}} :* {{ill|Sukunahikona|ja|少彦名命|1=Sukunabikona|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunahikona|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunabikona|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スクナビコナ}} :* {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素戔嗚命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}} :* {{ill|Ajisukitakahikone|ja|味耜高彦根命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ajisukitakahikone|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to アヂスキタカヒコネ}} :* {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q382005}}
== History == In the first year of [[:en:Emperor Kinmei|Emperor Kinmei]] (540 CE), {{ill|Shitateruhime|ja|下照比賣命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shitateruhime|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} is said to have descended to the southern area of Tamatsuoka, where a shrine called "Tamaoka no Yashiro" was established in her honor. Over the years, the shrine was renamed "Tamaoka Kasuga Shrine" and later "Hachioji Shrine" during the Edo period, eventually becoming known as Tamatsuoka Shrine.
In 1878, the main Hachioji Shrine (on the site of Tamaoka no Yashiro) consolidated five shrines: Hachioji Shrine (Tamaoka), Kasuga Shrine (Nishigaito), Tanaka Shrine (Miyanomae), Yasaka Shrine (Nishimaeda), and Tenjin Shrine (Tamanoi). The latter, Tenjin Shrine, originally known as Mukumoto Tenjin Shrine, was founded in 731 CE by [[:en:Tachibana no Moroe|Tachibana no Moroe]]<ref name="inui1993"/>.
The shrine grounds also include a Tachibana Shrine dedicated to Tachibana no Moroe and one of his descendants, [[:en:Kusunoki Masashige|Kusunoki Masashige]]. The shrine crest features a "flowing yamabuki (kerria)," which is associated with the family crest of Kusunoki Masashige. The main deity, Shitateruhime, is regarded as a "household harmony deity." The Edo-period main hall, constructed in the [[Kasuga-zukuri]] style, is a [[Kyoto Prefectural Cultural Property]]. The surrounding forest is also designated as a Cultural Property Environmental Preservation Area<ref>Historical Resource Survey Committee, History Exploration Division, *Ide Town History Tourism Guidebook – Walking Through Ide Village*, Ide Town Chamber of Commerce, 1995, p.9</ref>.

== Access == The shrine is approximately 2.0 kilometers from [[:en:Tamamizu Station|Tamamizu Station]] on the [[:en:JR Nara Line|JR Nara Line]], about a 30-minute walk<ref>[http://www.town.ide.kyoto.jp/rekishi_kanko/kankou_meisyo/1417411132279.html Tamatsuoka Shrine] Ide Town Website</ref>.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Tamatsuoka-jinja]][[ja:玉津岡神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q48763553]] Wikidata name: Tamatsuoka-jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:49, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Tamatsuoka Shrine (玉津岡神社) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:Tamatsuoka-jinja, keidai.jpg | Address = [[:en:Kyoto Prefecture|Kyoto Prefecture]], [[Tsuzuki District]], [[Ide Town]], Ide Higashigaito 63 | deity = {{ill|Shitateruhime|ja|下照比賣命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shitateruhime|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} | type = Former [[Gōsha]] | consecration_year = | Main Hall = | Other names = | Festivals = April 3, October 16 | Ceremonies = | Latitude Degrees = 34 | Latitude Minutes = 48 | Latitude Seconds = 15.1 | N/S = N | Longitude Degrees = 135 | Longitude Minutes = 49 | Longitude Seconds = 11.5 | E/W = E | Map Country Code = JP | Map = Japan Kyoto | Label Position = left | location = {{ill|Ide|WD=Q1203097}} | coordinates = {{Coord|34.804194|N|135.819861|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }} [[File:Tamatsuoka-jinja, torii.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|境内入り口}}]] '''Tamatsuoka Shrine''' (玉津岡神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[Ide Town]], [[:en:Kyoto Prefecture|Kyoto Prefecture]]. Its historical Rank under the [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|modern shrine Ranking system]] is that of a [[Gōsha]].
== Deities == The deities enshrined are as follows<ref name="inui1993">Koji Inui, *Ide Sadaijin Tachibana no Moroe and the Ide Township – The History and Culture of the Village of Ide*, Tamatsuoka Shrine, 1993, p.51</ref>:
; Main deity :* {{ill|Shitateruhime|ja|下照比賣命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shitateruhime|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
; Other Deities : The six deities of Tamatsuoka<ref>Masayoshi Ōnishi, *Tamatsuoka Shrine Summary*, Tamatsuoka Shrine, 1984, p.4</ref>: :* {{ill|Shitateruhime|ja|下照比賣命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shitateruhime|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} :* {{ill|Ame-no-Koyane|ja|天兒屋根命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Koyane|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天児屋命}} :* {{ill|Sukunahikona|ja|少彦名命|1=Sukunabikona|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunahikona|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunabikona|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スクナビコナ}} :* {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素戔嗚命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}} :* {{ill|Ajisukitakahikone|ja|味耜高彦根命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ajisukitakahikone|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to アヂスキタカヒコネ}} :* {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q382005}}
== History == In the first year of [[:en:Emperor Kinmei|Emperor Kinmei]] (540 CE), {{ill|Shitateruhime|ja|下照比賣命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shitateruhime|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} is said to have descended to the southern area of Tamatsuoka, where a shrine called "Tamaoka no Yashiro" was established in her honor. Over the years, the shrine was renamed "Tamaoka Kasuga Shrine" and later "Hachioji Shrine" during the Edo period, eventually becoming known as Tamatsuoka Shrine.
In 1878, the main Hachioji Shrine (on the site of Tamaoka no Yashiro) consolidated five shrines: Hachioji Shrine (Tamaoka), Kasuga Shrine (Nishigaito), Tanaka Shrine (Miyanomae), Yasaka Shrine (Nishimaeda), and Tenjin Shrine (Tamanoi). The latter, Tenjin Shrine, originally known as Mukumoto Tenjin Shrine, was founded in 731 CE by [[:en:Tachibana no Moroe|Tachibana no Moroe]]<ref name="inui1993"/>.
The shrine grounds also include a Tachibana Shrine dedicated to Tachibana no Moroe and one of his descendants, [[:en:Kusunoki Masashige|Kusunoki Masashige]]. The shrine crest features a "flowing yamabuki (kerria)," which is associated with the family crest of Kusunoki Masashige. The main deity, Shitateruhime, is regarded as a "household harmony deity." The Edo-period main hall, constructed in the [[Kasuga-zukuri]] style, is a [[Kyoto Prefectural Cultural Property]]. The surrounding forest is also designated as a Cultural Property Environmental Preservation Area<ref>Historical Resource Survey Committee, History Exploration Division, *Ide Town History Tourism Guidebook – Walking Through Ide Village*, Ide Town Chamber of Commerce, 1995, p.9</ref>.
== Grounds == <gallery> Tamatsuoka-jinja, honden.jpg|Main Hall (back right, Kyoto Prefectural Cultural Property) Tamatsuoka-jinja, nakamon.jpg|Inner Gate Tamatsuoka-jinja, haiden.jpg|Worship Hall Tamatsuoka-jinja, massha.jpg|Sub-shrine Daijingu (Kyoto Prefectural Cultural Property) </gallery>
== Cultural Properties == === Kyoto Prefectural Cultural Properties === * Tangible Cultural Properties: ''' Two structures at Tamatsuoka Shrine (Buildings) – Registered April 14, 1984: '''* Main Hall – Edo period '''* Sub-shrine Daijingu – Edo period
== Access == The shrine is approximately 2.0 kilometers from [[:en:Tamamizu Station|Tamamizu Station]] on the [[:en:JR Nara Line|JR Nara Line]], about a 30-minute walk<ref>[http://www.town.ide.kyoto.jp/rekishi_kanko/kankou_meisyo/1417411132279.html Tamatsuoka Shrine] Ide Town Website</ref>.

== History == In the first year of [[:en:Emperor Kinmei|Emperor Kinmei]] (540 CE), {{ill|Shitateruhime|ja|下照比賣命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shitateruhime|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} is said to have descended to the southern area of Tamatsuoka, where a shrine called "Tamaoka no Yashiro" was established in her honor. Over the years, the shrine was renamed "Tamaoka Kasuga Shrine" and later "Hachioji Shrine" during the Edo period, eventually becoming known as Tamatsuoka Shrine.
In 1878, the main Hachioji Shrine (on the site of Tamaoka no Yashiro) consolidated five shrines: Hachioji Shrine (Tamaoka), Kasuga Shrine (Nishigaito), Tanaka Shrine (Miyanomae), Yasaka Shrine (Nishimaeda), and Tenjin Shrine (Tamanoi). The latter, Tenjin Shrine, originally known as Mukumoto Tenjin Shrine, was founded in 731 CE by [[:en:Tachibana no Moroe|Tachibana no Moroe]]<ref name="inui1993"/>.
The shrine grounds also include a Tachibana Shrine dedicated to Tachibana no Moroe and one of his descendants, [[:en:Kusunoki Masashige|Kusunoki Masashige]]. The shrine crest features a "flowing yamabuki (kerria)," which is associated with the family crest of Kusunoki Masashige. The main deity, Shitateruhime, is regarded as a "household harmony deity." The Edo-period main hall, constructed in the [[Kasuga-zukuri]] style, is a [[Kyoto Prefectural Cultural Property]]. The surrounding forest is also designated as a Cultural Property Environmental Preservation Area<ref>Historical Resource Survey Committee, History Exploration Division, *Ide Town History Tourism Guidebook – Walking Through Ide Village*, Ide Town Chamber of Commerce, 1995, p.9</ref>.

== History == In the first year of [[:en:Emperor Kinmei|Emperor Kinmei]] (540 CE), {{ill|Shitateruhime|ja|下照比賣命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shitateruhime|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} is said to have descended to the southern area of Tamatsuoka, where a shrine called "Tamaoka no Yashiro" was established in her honor. Over the years, the shrine was renamed "Tamaoka Kasuga Shrine" and later "Hachioji Shrine" during the Edo period, eventually becoming known as Tamatsuoka Shrine.
In 1878, the main Hachioji Shrine (on the site of Tamaoka no Yashiro) consolidated five shrines: Hachioji Shrine (Tamaoka), Kasuga Shrine (Nishigaito), Tanaka Shrine (Miyanomae), Yasaka Shrine (Nishimaeda), and Tenjin Shrine (Tamanoi). The latter, Tenjin Shrine, originally known as Mukumoto Tenjin Shrine, was founded in 731 CE by [[:en:Tachibana no Moroe|Tachibana no Moroe]]<ref name="inui1993"/>.
The shrine grounds also include a Tachibana Shrine dedicated to Tachibana no Moroe and one of his descendants, [[:en:Kusunoki Masashige|Kusunoki Masashige]]. The shrine crest features a "flowing yamabuki (kerria)," which is associated with the family crest of Kusunoki Masashige. The main deity, Shitateruhime, is regarded as a "household harmony deity." The Edo-period main hall, constructed in the [[Kasuga-zukuri]] style, is a [[Kyoto Prefectural Cultural Property]]. The surrounding forest is also designated as a Cultural Property Environmental Preservation Area<ref>Historical Resource Survey Committee, History Exploration Division, *Ide Town History Tourism Guidebook – Walking Through Ide Village*, Ide Town Chamber of Commerce, 1995, p.9</ref>.

== Access == The shrine is approximately 2.0 kilometers from [[:en:Tamamizu Station|Tamamizu Station]] on the [[:en:JR Nara Line|JR Nara Line]], about a 30-minute walk<ref>[http://www.town.ide.kyoto.jp/rekishi_kanko/kankou_meisyo/1417411132279.html Tamatsuoka Shrine] Ide Town Website</ref>.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[commons:Category:Tamatsuoka-jinja]][[ja:玉津岡神社]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[commons:Category:Tamatsuoka-jinja]][[ja:玉津岡神社]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Tamatsuoka-jinja]][[ja:玉津岡神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q48763553]] Wikidata name: Tamatsuoka-jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:49, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Tamatsuoka Shrine (玉津岡神社) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:Tamatsuoka-jinja, keidai.jpg | Address = [[:en:Kyoto Prefecture|Kyoto Prefecture]], [[Tsuzuki District]], [[Ide Town]], Ide Higashigaito 63 | deity = {{ill|Shitateruhime|ja|下照比賣命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shitateruhime|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} | type = Former [[Gōsha]] | consecration_year = | Main Hall = | Other names = | Festivals = April 3, October 16 | Ceremonies = | Latitude Degrees = 34 | Latitude Minutes = 48 | Latitude Seconds = 15.1 | N/S = N | Longitude Degrees = 135 | Longitude Minutes = 49 | Longitude Seconds = 11.5 | E/W = E | Map Country Code = JP | Map = Japan Kyoto | Label Position = left | location = {{ill|Ide|WD=Q1203097}} | coordinates = {{Coord|34.804194|N|135.819861|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }} [[File:Tamatsuoka-jinja, torii.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|境内入り口}}]] '''Tamatsuoka Shrine''' (玉津岡神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[Ide Town]], [[:en:Kyoto Prefecture|Kyoto Prefecture]]. Its historical Rank under the [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|modern shrine Ranking system]] is that of a [[Gōsha]].
== Deities == The deities enshrined are as follows<ref name="inui1993">Koji Inui, *Ide Sadaijin Tachibana no Moroe and the Ide Township – The History and Culture of the Village of Ide*, Tamatsuoka Shrine, 1993, p.51</ref>:
; Main deity :* {{ill|Shitateruhime|ja|下照比賣命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shitateruhime|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
; Other Deities : The six deities of Tamatsuoka<ref>Masayoshi Ōnishi, *Tamatsuoka Shrine Summary*, Tamatsuoka Shrine, 1984, p.4</ref>: :* {{ill|Shitateruhime|ja|下照比賣命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shitateruhime|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} :* {{ill|Ame-no-Koyane|ja|天兒屋根命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Koyane|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天児屋命}} :* {{ill|Sukunahikona|ja|少彦名命|1=Sukunabikona|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunahikona|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunabikona|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スクナビコナ}} :* {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素戔嗚命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}} :* {{ill|Ajisukitakahikone|ja|味耜高彦根命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ajisukitakahikone|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to アヂスキタカヒコネ}} :* {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q382005}}
== History == In the first year of [[:en:Emperor Kinmei|Emperor Kinmei]] (540 CE), {{ill|Shitateruhime|ja|下照比賣命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shitateruhime|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} is said to have descended to the southern area of Tamatsuoka, where a shrine called "Tamaoka no Yashiro" was established in her honor. Over the years, the shrine was renamed "Tamaoka Kasuga Shrine" and later "Hachioji Shrine" during the Edo period, eventually becoming known as Tamatsuoka Shrine.
In 1878, the main Hachioji Shrine (on the site of Tamaoka no Yashiro) consolidated five shrines: Hachioji Shrine (Tamaoka), Kasuga Shrine (Nishigaito), Tanaka Shrine (Miyanomae), Yasaka Shrine (Nishimaeda), and Tenjin Shrine (Tamanoi). The latter, Tenjin Shrine, originally known as Mukumoto Tenjin Shrine, was founded in 731 CE by [[:en:Tachibana no Moroe|Tachibana no Moroe]]<ref name="inui1993"/>.
The shrine grounds also include a Tachibana Shrine dedicated to Tachibana no Moroe and one of his descendants, [[:en:Kusunoki Masashige|Kusunoki Masashige]]. The shrine crest features a "flowing yamabuki (kerria)," which is associated with the family crest of Kusunoki Masashige. The main deity, Shitateruhime, is regarded as a "household harmony deity." The Edo-period main hall, constructed in the [[Kasuga-zukuri]] style, is a [[Kyoto Prefectural Cultural Property]]. The surrounding forest is also designated as a Cultural Property Environmental Preservation Area<ref>Historical Resource Survey Committee, History Exploration Division, *Ide Town History Tourism Guidebook – Walking Through Ide Village*, Ide Town Chamber of Commerce, 1995, p.9</ref>.
== Grounds == <gallery> Tamatsuoka-jinja, honden.jpg|Main Hall (back right, Kyoto Prefectural Cultural Property) Tamatsuoka-jinja, nakamon.jpg|Inner Gate Tamatsuoka-jinja, haiden.jpg|Worship Hall Tamatsuoka-jinja, massha.jpg|Sub-shrine Daijingu (Kyoto Prefectural Cultural Property) </gallery>
== Cultural Properties == === Kyoto Prefectural Cultural Properties === * Tangible Cultural Properties: ''' Two structures at Tamatsuoka Shrine (Buildings) – Registered April 14, 1984: '''* Main Hall – Edo period '''* Sub-shrine Daijingu – Edo period
== Access == The shrine is approximately 2.0 kilometers from [[:en:Tamamizu Station|Tamamizu Station]] on the [[:en:JR Nara Line|JR Nara Line]], about a 30-minute walk<ref>[http://www.town.ide.kyoto.jp/rekishi_kanko/kankou_meisyo/1417411132279.html Tamatsuoka Shrine] Ide Town Website</ref>.

== History == In the first year of [[:en:Emperor Kinmei|Emperor Kinmei]] (540 CE), {{ill|Shitateruhime|ja|下照比賣命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shitateruhime|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} is said to have descended to the southern area of Tamatsuoka, where a shrine called "Tamaoka no Yashiro" was established in her honor. The surrounding forest is also designated as a Cultural Property Environmental Preservation Area<ref>Historical Resource Survey Committee, History Exploration Division, *Ide Town History Tourism Guidebook – Walking Through Ide Village*, Ide Town Chamber of Commerce, 1995, p.9</ref>.

; Other Deities : The six deities of Tamatsuoka<ref>Masayoshi Ōnishi, *Tamatsuoka Shrine Summary*, Tamatsuoka Shrine, 1984, p.4</ref>: :* {{ill|Shitateruhime|ja|下照比賣命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shitateruhime|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} :* {{ill|Ame-no-Koyane|ja|天兒屋根命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Koyane|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天児屋命}} :* {{ill|Sukunahikona|ja|少彦名命|1=Sukunabikona|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunahikona|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunabikona|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スクナビコナ}} :* {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素戔嗚命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}} :* {{ill|Ajisukitakahikone|ja|味耜高彦根命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ajisukitakahikone|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to アヂスキタカヒコネ}} :* {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q382005}}
== History == In the first year of [[:en:Emperor Kinmei|Emperor Kinmei]] (540 CE), {{ill|Shitateruhime|ja|下照比賣命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shitateruhime|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} is said to have descended to the southern area of Tamatsuoka, where a shrine called "Tamaoka no Yashiro" was established in her honor. Over the years, the shrine was renamed "Tamaoka Kasuga Shrine" and later "Hachioji Shrine" during the Edo period, eventually becoming known as Tamatsuoka Shrine.
In 1878, the main Hachioji Shrine (on the site of Tamaoka no Yashiro) consolidated five shrines: Hachioji Shrine (Tamaoka), Kasuga Shrine (Nishigaito), Tanaka Shrine (Miyanomae), Yasaka Shrine (Nishimaeda), and Tenjin Shrine (Tamanoi). The latter, Tenjin Shrine, originally known as Mukumoto Tenjin Shrine, was founded in 731 CE by [[:en:Tachibana no Moroe|Tachibana no Moroe]]<ref name="inui1993"/>.
The shrine grounds also include a Tachibana Shrine dedicated to Tachibana no Moroe and one of his descendants, [[:en:Kusunoki Masashige|Kusunoki Masashige]]. The shrine crest features a "flowing yamabuki (kerria)," which is associated with the family crest of Kusunoki Masashige. The main deity, Shitateruhime, is regarded as a "household harmony deity." The Edo-period main hall, constructed in the [[Kasuga-zukuri]] style, is a [[Kyoto Prefectural Cultural Property]]. The surrounding forest is also designated as a Cultural Property Environmental Preservation Area<ref>Historical Resource Survey Committee, History Exploration Division, *Ide Town History Tourism Guidebook – Walking Through Ide Village*, Ide Town Chamber of Commerce, 1995, p.9</ref>.

The Edo-period main hall, constructed in the [[Kasuga-zukuri]] style, is a [[Kyoto Prefectural Cultural Property]]. The surrounding forest is also designated as a Cultural Property Environmental Preservation Area<ref>Historical Resource Survey Committee, History Exploration Division, *Ide Town History Tourism Guidebook – Walking Through Ide Village*, Ide Town Chamber of Commerce, 1995, p.9</ref>.
== Grounds == <gallery> Tamatsuoka-jinja, honden.jpg|Main Hall (back right, Kyoto Prefectural Cultural Property) Tamatsuoka-jinja, nakamon.jpg|Inner Gate Tamatsuoka-jinja, haiden.jpg|Worship Hall Tamatsuoka-jinja, massha.jpg|Sub-shrine Daijingu (Kyoto Prefectural Cultural Property) </gallery>

== Access == The shrine is approximately 2.0 kilometers from {{ill|Tamamizu Station|en|Tamamizu Station|WD=Q1049014|lt=Tamamizu Station}} on the [[:en:JR Nara Line|JR Nara Line]], about a 30-minute walk<ref>[http://www.town.ide.kyoto.jp/rekishi_kanko/kankou_meisyo/1417411132279.html Tamatsuoka Shrine] Ide Town Website</ref>.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Tamatsuoka-jinja]][[ja:玉津岡神社]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Tamatsuoka-jinja]][[ja:玉津岡神社]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Tamatsuoka-jinja]][[ja:玉津岡神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q48763553]] Wikidata name: Tamatsuoka-jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:49, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Tamatsuoka Shrine (玉津岡神社) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:Tamatsuoka-jinja, keidai.jpg | Address = {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|en|Kyoto Prefecture|WD=Q120730|lt=Kyoto Prefecture}}, [[Tsuzuki District]], [[Ide Town]], Ide Higashigaito 63 | deity = {{ill|Shitateruhime|ja|下照比賣命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shitateruhime|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} | type = Former [[Gōsha]] | consecration_year = | Main Hall = | Other names = | Festivals = April 3, October 16 | Ceremonies = | Latitude Degrees = 34 | Latitude Minutes = 48 | Latitude Seconds = 15.1 | N/S = N | Longitude Degrees = 135 | Longitude Minutes = 49 | Longitude Seconds = 11.5 | E/W = E | Map Country Code = JP | Map = Japan Kyoto | Label Position = left | location = {{ill|Ide|WD=Q1203097}} | coordinates = {{Coord|34.804194|N|135.819861|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }} [[File:Tamatsuoka-jinja, torii.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|境内入り口}}]] '''Tamatsuoka Shrine''' (玉津岡神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}} located in [[Ide Town]], {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|en|Kyoto Prefecture|WD=Q120730|lt=Kyoto Prefecture}}. Its historical Rank under the [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|modern shrine Ranking system]] is that of a [[Gōsha]].
== Deities == The deities enshrined are as follows<ref name="inui1993">Koji Inui, *Ide Sadaijin Tachibana no Moroe and the Ide Township – The History and Culture of the Village of Ide*, Tamatsuoka Shrine, 1993, p.51</ref>:
; Main deity :* {{ill|Shitateruhime|ja|下照比賣命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shitateruhime|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
; Other Deities : The six deities of Tamatsuoka<ref>Masayoshi Ōnishi, *Tamatsuoka Shrine Summary*, Tamatsuoka Shrine, 1984, p.4</ref>: :* {{ill|Shitateruhime|ja|下照比賣命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shitateruhime|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} :* {{ill|Ame-no-Koyane|ja|天兒屋根命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Koyane|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天児屋命}} :* {{ill|Sukunahikona|ja|少彦名命|1=Sukunabikona|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunahikona|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunabikona|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スクナビコナ}} :* {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素戔嗚命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}} :* {{ill|Ajisukitakahikone|ja|味耜高彦根命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ajisukitakahikone|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to アヂスキタカヒコネ}} :* {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q382005}}
== History == In the first year of {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|en|Emperor Kinmei|WD=Q329792|lt=Emperor Kinmei}} (540 CE), {{ill|Shitateruhime|ja|下照比賣命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shitateruhime|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} is said to have descended to the southern area of Tamatsuoka, where a shrine called "Tamaoka no Yashiro" was established in her honor. Over the years, the shrine was renamed "Tamaoka Kasuga Shrine" and later "Hachioji Shrine" during the Edo period, eventually becoming known as Tamatsuoka Shrine.
In 1878, the main Hachioji Shrine (on the site of Tamaoka no Yashiro) consolidated five shrines: Hachioji Shrine (Tamaoka), Kasuga Shrine (Nishigaito), Tanaka Shrine (Miyanomae), Yasaka Shrine (Nishimaeda), and Tenjin Shrine (Tamanoi). The latter, Tenjin Shrine, originally known as Mukumoto Tenjin Shrine, was founded in 731 CE by {{ill|Tachibana no Moroe|en|Tachibana no Moroe|WD=Q3135863|lt=Tachibana no Moroe}}<ref name="inui1993"/>.
The shrine grounds also include a Tachibana Shrine dedicated to Tachibana no Moroe and one of his descendants, {{ill|Kusunoki Masashige|en|Kusunoki Masashige|WD=Q382234|lt=Kusunoki Masashige}}. The shrine crest features a "flowing yamabuki (kerria)," which is associated with the family crest of Kusunoki Masashige. The main deity, Shitateruhime, is regarded as a "household harmony deity." The Edo-period main hall, constructed in the [[Kasuga-zukuri]] style, is a [[Kyoto Prefectural Cultural Property]]. The surrounding forest is also designated as a Cultural Property Environmental Preservation Area<ref>Historical Resource Survey Committee, History Exploration Division, *Ide Town History Tourism Guidebook – Walking Through Ide Village*, Ide Town Chamber of Commerce, 1995, p.9</ref>.
== Grounds == <gallery> Tamatsuoka-jinja, honden.jpg|Main Hall (back right, Kyoto Prefectural Cultural Property) Tamatsuoka-jinja, nakamon.jpg|Inner Gate Tamatsuoka-jinja, haiden.jpg|Worship Hall Tamatsuoka-jinja, massha.jpg|Sub-shrine Daijingu (Kyoto Prefectural Cultural Property) </gallery>
== Cultural Properties == === Kyoto Prefectural Cultural Properties === * Tangible Cultural Properties: ''' Two structures at Tamatsuoka Shrine (Buildings) – Registered April 14, 1984: '''* Main Hall – Edo period '''* Sub-shrine Daijingu – Edo period
== Access == The shrine is approximately 2.0 kilometers from {{ill|Tamamizu Station|en|Tamamizu Station|WD=Q1049014|lt=Tamamizu Station}} on the [[:en:JR Nara Line|JR Nara Line]], about a 30-minute walk<ref>[http://www.town.ide.kyoto.jp/rekishi_kanko/kankou_meisyo/1417411132279.html Tamatsuoka Shrine] Ide Town Website</ref>.

Its historical Rank under the [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|modern shrine Ranking system]] is that of a [[Gōsha]].
== Deities == The deities enshrined are as follows<ref name="inui1993">Koji Inui, *Ide Sadaijin Tachibana no Moroe and the Ide Township – The History and Culture of the Village of Ide*, Tamatsuoka Shrine, 1993, p.51</ref>:

== History == In the first year of {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|en|Emperor Kinmei|WD=Q329792|lt=Emperor Kinmei}} (540 CE), {{ill|Shitateruhime|ja|下照比賣命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shitateruhime|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} is said to have descended to the southern area of Tamatsuoka, where a shrine called "Tamaoka no Yashiro" was established in her honor. The latter, Tenjin Shrine, originally known as Mukumoto Tenjin Shrine, was founded in 731 CE by {{ill|Tachibana no Moroe|en|Tachibana no Moroe|WD=Q3135863|lt=Tachibana no Moroe}}<ref name="inui1993"/>.
The shrine grounds also include a Tachibana Shrine dedicated to Tachibana no Moroe and one of his descendants, {{ill|Kusunoki Masashige|en|Kusunoki Masashige|WD=Q382234|lt=Kusunoki Masashige}}.
