{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Tamatsuoka Shrine (玉津岡神社) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:Tamatsuoka-jinja, keidai.jpg | Address = {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|en|Kyoto Prefecture|WD=Q120730|lt=Kyoto Prefecture}}, [[Tsuzuki District]], [[Ide Town]], Ide Higashigaito 63 | deity = {{ill|Shitateruhime|ja|下照比賣命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shitateruhime|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} | type = Former [[Gōsha]] | consecration_year = | Main Hall = | Other names = | Festivals = April 3, October 16 | Ceremonies = | Latitude Degrees = 34 | Latitude Minutes = 48 | Latitude Seconds = 15.1 | N/S = N | Longitude Degrees = 135 | Longitude Minutes = 49 | Longitude Seconds = 11.5 | E/W = E | Map Country Code = JP | Map = Japan Kyoto | Label Position = left | location = {{ill|Ide|WD=Q1203097}} | coordinates = {{Coord|34.804194|N|135.819861|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }} [[File:Tamatsuoka-jinja, torii.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|境内入り口}}]] '''Tamatsuoka Shrine''' (玉津岡神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}} located in [[Ide Town]], {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|en|Kyoto Prefecture|WD=Q120730|lt=Kyoto Prefecture}}. Its historical Rank under the [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|modern shrine Ranking system]] is that of a [[Gōsha]].
== Deities == The deities enshrined are as follows<ref name="inui1993">Koji Inui, *Ide Sadaijin Tachibana no Moroe and the Ide Township – The History and Culture of the Village of Ide*, Tamatsuoka Shrine, 1993, p.51</ref>:
; Main deity :* {{ill|Shitateruhime|ja|下照比賣命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shitateruhime|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
; Other Deities : The six deities of Tamatsuoka<ref>Masayoshi Ōnishi, *Tamatsuoka Shrine Summary*, Tamatsuoka Shrine, 1984, p.4</ref>: :* {{ill|Shitateruhime|ja|下照比賣命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shitateruhime|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} :* {{ill|Ame-no-Koyane|ja|天兒屋根命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Koyane|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天児屋命}} :* {{ill|Sukunahikona|ja|少彦名命|1=Sukunabikona|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunahikona|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunabikona|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スクナビコナ}} :* {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素戔嗚命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}} :* {{ill|Ajisukitakahikone|ja|味耜高彦根命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ajisukitakahikone|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to アヂスキタカヒコネ}} :* {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q382005}}
== History == In the first year of {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|en|Emperor Kinmei|WD=Q329792|lt=Emperor Kinmei}} (540 CE), {{ill|Shitateruhime|ja|下照比賣命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shitateruhime|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} is said to have descended to the southern area of Tamatsuoka, where a shrine called "Tamaoka no Yashiro" was established in her honor. Over the years, the shrine was renamed "Tamaoka Kasuga Shrine" and later "Hachioji Shrine" during the Edo period, eventually becoming known as Tamatsuoka Shrine.
In 1878, the main Hachioji Shrine (on the site of Tamaoka no Yashiro) consolidated five shrines: Hachioji Shrine (Tamaoka), Kasuga Shrine (Nishigaito), Tanaka Shrine (Miyanomae), Yasaka Shrine (Nishimaeda), and Tenjin Shrine (Tamanoi). The latter, Tenjin Shrine, originally known as Mukumoto Tenjin Shrine, was founded in 731 CE by {{ill|Tachibana no Moroe|en|Tachibana no Moroe|WD=Q3135863|lt=Tachibana no Moroe}}<ref name="inui1993"/>.
The shrine grounds also include a Tachibana Shrine dedicated to Tachibana no Moroe and one of his descendants, {{ill|Kusunoki Masashige|en|Kusunoki Masashige|WD=Q382234|lt=Kusunoki Masashige}}. The shrine crest features a "flowing yamabuki (kerria)," which is associated with the family crest of Kusunoki Masashige. The main deity, Shitateruhime, is regarded as a "household harmony deity." The Edo-period main hall, constructed in the [[Kasuga-zukuri]] style, is a [[Kyoto Prefectural Cultural Property]]. The surrounding forest is also designated as a Cultural Property Environmental Preservation Area<ref>Historical Resource Survey Committee, History Exploration Division, *Ide Town History Tourism Guidebook – Walking Through Ide Village*, Ide Town Chamber of Commerce, 1995, p.9</ref>.
== Grounds == <gallery> Tamatsuoka-jinja, honden.jpg|Main Hall (back right, Kyoto Prefectural Cultural Property) Tamatsuoka-jinja, nakamon.jpg|Inner Gate Tamatsuoka-jinja, haiden.jpg|Worship Hall Tamatsuoka-jinja, massha.jpg|Sub-shrine Daijingu (Kyoto Prefectural Cultural Property) </gallery>
== Cultural Properties == === Kyoto Prefectural Cultural Properties === * Tangible Cultural Properties: ''' Two structures at Tamatsuoka Shrine (Buildings) – Registered April 14, 1984: '''* Main Hall – Edo period '''* Sub-shrine Daijingu – Edo period
== Access == The shrine is approximately 2.0 kilometers from {{ill|Tamamizu Station|en|Tamamizu Station|WD=Q1049014|lt=Tamamizu Station}} on the [[:en:JR Nara Line|JR Nara Line]], about a 30-minute walk<ref>[http://www.town.ide.kyoto.jp/rekishi_kanko/kankou_meisyo/1417411132279.html Tamatsuoka Shrine] Ide Town Website</ref>.

== External Links == * {{Commonscat-inline|Tamatsuoka-jinja}}

; Other Deities : The six deities of Tamatsuoka<ref>Masayoshi Ōnishi, *Tamatsuoka Shrine Summary*, Tamatsuoka Shrine, 1984, p.4</ref>: :* {{ill|Shitateruhime|ja|下照比賣命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shitateruhime|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} :* {{ill|Ame-no-Koyane|ja|天兒屋根命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Koyane|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天児屋命}} :* {{ill|Sukunahikona|ja|少彦名命|1=Sukunabikona|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunahikona|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunabikona|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スクナビコナ}} :* {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素戔嗚命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}} :* {{ill|Ajisukitakahikone|ja|味耜高彦根命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ajisukitakahikone|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to アヂスキタカヒコネ}} :* {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q382005}}
== History == In the first year of {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|en|Emperor Kinmei|WD=Q329792|lt=Emperor Kinmei|1=Emperor Kinmei}} (540 CE), {{ill|Shitateruhime|ja|下照比賣命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shitateruhime|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} is said to have descended to the southern area of Tamatsuoka, where a shrine called "Tamaoka no Yashiro" was established in her honor. Over the years, the shrine was renamed "Tamaoka Kasuga Shrine" and later "Hachioji Shrine" during the Edo period, eventually becoming known as Tamatsuoka Shrine.
In 1878, the main Hachioji Shrine (on the site of Tamaoka no Yashiro) consolidated five shrines: Hachioji Shrine (Tamaoka), Kasuga Shrine (Nishigaito), Tanaka Shrine (Miyanomae), Yasaka Shrine (Nishimaeda), and Tenjin Shrine (Tamanoi). The latter, Tenjin Shrine, originally known as Mukumoto Tenjin Shrine, was founded in 731 CE by {{ill|Tachibana no Moroe|en|Tachibana no Moroe|WD=Q3135863|lt=Tachibana no Moroe|1=Tachibana no Moroe}}<ref name="inui1993"/>.
The shrine grounds also include a Tachibana Shrine dedicated to Tachibana no Moroe and one of his descendants, {{ill|Kusunoki Masashige|en|Kusunoki Masashige|WD=Q382234|lt=Kusunoki Masashige|1=Kusunoki Masashige}}. The shrine crest features a "flowing yamabuki (kerria)," which is associated with the family crest of Kusunoki Masashige. The main deity, Shitateruhime, is regarded as a "household harmony deity." The Edo-period main hall, constructed in the [[Kasuga-zukuri]] style, is a [[Kyoto Prefectural Cultural Property]]. The surrounding forest is also designated as a Cultural Property Environmental Preservation Area<ref>Historical Resource Survey Committee, History Exploration Division, *Ide Town History Tourism Guidebook – Walking Through Ide Village*, Ide Town Chamber of Commerce, 1995, p.9</ref>.
== Grounds == <gallery> Tamatsuoka-jinja, honden.jpg|Main Hall (back right, Kyoto Prefectural Cultural Property) Tamatsuoka-jinja, nakamon.jpg|Inner Gate Tamatsuoka-jinja, haiden.jpg|Worship Hall Tamatsuoka-jinja, massha.jpg|Sub-shrine Daijingu (Kyoto Prefectural Cultural Property) </gallery>
== Cultural Properties == === Kyoto Prefectural Cultural Properties === * Tangible Cultural Properties: ''' Two structures at Tamatsuoka Shrine (Buildings) – Registered April 14, 1984: '''* Main Hall – Edo period '''* Sub-shrine Daijingu – Edo period
== Access == The shrine is approximately 2.0 kilometers from {{ill|Tamamizu Station|en|Tamamizu Station|WD=Q1049014|lt=Tamamizu Station|1=Tamamizu Station}} on the [[:en:JR Nara Line|JR Nara Line]], about a 30-minute walk<ref>[http://www.town.ide.kyoto.jp/rekishi_kanko/kankou_meisyo/1417411132279.html Tamatsuoka Shrine] Ide Town Website</ref>.

Over the years, the shrine was renamed "Tamaoka Kasuga Shrine" and later "Hachioji Shrine" during the Edo period, eventually becoming known as Tamatsuoka Shrine.
In 1878, the main Hachioji Shrine (on the site of Tamaoka no Yashiro) consolidated five shrines: Hachioji Shrine (Tamaoka), Kasuga Shrine (Nishigaito), Tanaka Shrine (Miyanomae), Yasaka Shrine (Nishimaeda), and Tenjin Shrine (Tamanoi). The latter, Tenjin Shrine, originally known as Mukumoto Tenjin Shrine, was founded in 731 CE by {{ill|Tachibana no Moroe|en|Tachibana no Moroe|WD=Q3135863|lt=Tachibana no Moroe|1=Tachibana no Moroe}}<ref name="inui1993"/>.
The shrine grounds also include a Tachibana Shrine dedicated to Tachibana no Moroe and one of his descendants, {{ill|Kusunoki Masashige|en|Kusunoki Masashige|WD=Q382234|lt=Kusunoki Masashige|1=Kusunoki Masashige}}. The shrine crest features a "flowing yamabuki (kerria)," which is associated with the family crest of Kusunoki Masashige. The main deity, Shitateruhime, is regarded as a "household harmony deity." The Edo-period main hall, constructed in the [[Kasuga-zukuri]] style, is a [[Kyoto Prefectural Cultural Property]]. The surrounding forest is also designated as a Cultural Property Environmental Preservation Area<ref>Historical Resource Survey Committee, History Exploration Division, *Ide Town History Tourism Guidebook – Walking Through Ide Village*, Ide Town Chamber of Commerce, 1995, p.9</ref>.

== Cultural Properties == === Kyoto Prefectural Cultural Properties === * Tangible Cultural Properties: ''' Two structures at Tamatsuoka Shrine (Buildings) – Registered April 14, 1984: '''* Main Hall – Edo period '''* Sub-shrine Daijingu – Edo period
== Access == The shrine is approximately 2.0 kilometers from {{ill|Tamamizu Station|en|Tamamizu Station|WD=Q1049014|lt=Tamamizu Station|1=Tamamizu Station}} on the [[:en:JR Nara Line|JR Nara Line]], about a 30-minute walk<ref>[http://www.town.ide.kyoto.jp/rekishi_kanko/kankou_meisyo/1417411132279.html Tamatsuoka Shrine] Ide Town Website</ref>.
